headline: Health Privatization Debate Reignited by 3 Hospital PPP Proposals
context: Three new public-private partnership hospital proposals in 2025 revived debate over healthcare privatization risks.

cards.big: 3 new PPP hospital projects worth RM4.5 billion proposed in Klang Valley alone
cards.sub: Critics warn privatization creep will erode universal public healthcare access

cards.big: Existing PPP hospitals charge patients 15-30% more than fully public facilities
cards.sub: Cost premiums mean PPP hospitals serve middle income while B40 remain in overcrowded public wards

cards.big: Article 5 of the Federal Constitution guarantees right to life interpreted to include healthcare
cards.sub: Legal scholars debate whether privatization conflicts with constitutional health obligations

cards.big: UK's PFI hospital program cost NHS 50% more than conventional public procurement
cards.sub: Long-term PPP costs often exceed initial savings due to profit margins and contract terms

cards.big: Healthcare privatization does not improve efficiency—it redirects public money to private profits
Like outsourcing school lunches to save money and then wondering why the portions shrink — efficiency gains flow to the operator, not the students
cards.sub: Healthcare PPPs redirect public funding through private profit structures without guaranteed service improvements

cards.big: Should Malaysia ban PPP models for essential hospital services in high-demand areas
